name: Cloudflare Workers
what_it_does: Cloudflare's edge compute platform running JavaScript/WASM on 300+ global PoPs with sub-millisecond cold starts, KV store, Durable Objects, and AI Gateway.
use_cases: Globally distributed AI agent functions with zero cold start latency at Cloudflare edge
Request routing and middleware for AI APIs using Workers as intelligent proxy layer
Stateful agent coordination using Durable Objects for consistent global state
Edge-side caching and rate limiting for LLM API calls using Workers KV
Cloudflare AI Gateway for LLM request logging, caching, and fallback routing
not_for: Long-running compute beyond 30 seconds (CPU time limit, not wall time)
Node.js-specific packages or native addons (V8 isolates, not Node.js)
Disk I/O or local filesystem access (stateless ephemeral compute)
best_when: Building globally distributed agents or middleware that needs near-zero latency at the edge, or using Cloudflare AI Gateway for LLM proxy capabilities.
avoid_when: You need heavy Node.js dependencies, long-running jobs, or are not already managing DNS/CDN through Cloudflare.

auth.notes: API tokens with fine-grained permissions for deployments. Workers themselves use service bindings or secrets for downstream auth.

pricing.paid_tiers: Workers Paid: $5/month for 10M requests, then $0.30/million; Durable Objects: $0.15/million requests
pricing.notes: Very generous free tier for most use cases. KV storage priced separately. AI Gateway is free for basic features.

performance.rate_limits: Free: 1000 requests/minute; Paid: effectively unlimited with burst protection

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Wrangler CLI provides detailed deployment errors; runtime errors need Workers logging or tail for debugging
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Durable Objects support transactional storage for idempotent operations
known_agent_gotchas: CPU time limit (10ms free, 30s paid) is CPU time not wall clock — blocking I/O doesn't count but compute does
No Node.js built-ins — require('fs'), require('path'), etc. don't exist; use Web APIs (fetch, TextEncoder) instead
Durable Objects have single-threaded semantics per object ID — design for sequential not concurrent access within one DO
Workers KV is eventually consistent (up to 60s lag globally) — not suitable for real-time coordination without Durable Objects
Cold starts are ~0ms for most Workers but first request after long idle (hours) may see 5-50ms initialization

security_score_components.security_notes: V8 isolates provide strong sandboxing between Workers. Secrets bound to Worker at deploy time, never in logs. API tokens scoped to zone/account.
